mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 6.5%, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger recession fears, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day forward returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 6.5%.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising housing starts and contracting industrial production indicates a structural shift where capital expenditure moves from manufacturing to real estate, reducing corporate profit margins and pressuring equity prices over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising HOUST and falling INDPRO fails to predict negative 5-day SPX returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, real consumer credit growth becomes the primary driver of corporate revenue expectations; accelerating credit growth sustains equity valuations over a 20-day horizon by confirming resilient aggregate demand.
failure_condition: Forward returns of RSAFS growth do not correlate positively with SPX 20d returns when CPIAUCSL exceeds 230.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly contraction in real GDP growth below 1.5% annualized rate signals an imminent recession, causing equity markets to price in lower earnings expectations and generating negative forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the negative GDP signal.

mechanism_narrative: When strong employment growth coincides with elevated federal funds rates, the labor market resilience prevents a soft landing, forcing the Fed to maintain restrictive policy longer than expected, which compresses equity valuations over a 5-day horizon.
regime_hint: restrictive monetary policy with resilient labor
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 5-day SPX returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production reflects expanding corporate capacity and healthy supply-side dynamics, which directly supports earnings revisions and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns in growth periods do not significantly exceed baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in real consumer credit exceeding 2% signals a sudden pullback in household spending, which transmits to corporate revenues and depresses equity prices over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when RSAFS declines exceed 2% in more than 70% of historical instances.
